html:
Payroll software pricing in Jamaica varies widely depending on the provider, the number of employees, and the features included. This guide breaks down what you should actually expect to pay — and how to evaluate whether the cost is justified for your business.

Why Payroll Software Is Different from Generic Accounting Software

Payroll in Jamaica is not just bookkeeping. It requires real-time compliance with PAYE thresholds, NIS ceilings, NHT rates, and Education Tax rates — all of which change when TAJ or the Ministry of Finance issues updates. Software that isn't built specifically for Jamaica often lacks:

  • The correct J$1,500,096/year PAYE threshold (2026)
  • The NIS annual ceiling of J$5,000,000
  • Education Tax at 2.25% (employee) + 3.5% (employer)
  • NHT at 2% (employee) + 3% (employer) with no ceiling
  • S01 monthly remittance report format acceptable to TAJ

Generic accounting tools like QuickBooks or Xero require significant manual configuration to handle Jamaica payroll correctly — and none generate the TAJ-compliant S01 form automatically.

What Payroll Software Typically Costs in Jamaica

Per-Employee Monthly Pricing (Most Common Model)

EmployeesLow EndMid RangeHigh End
1–5J$2,000–4,000/moJ$4,000–7,000/moJ$8,000–15,000/mo
6–15J$4,500–8,000/moJ$8,000–15,000/moJ$15,000–30,000/mo
16–30J$8,000–14,000/moJ$14,000–25,000/moJ$25,000–50,000/mo
31–50J$12,000–20,000/moJ$20,000–35,000/moJ$35,000–70,000/mo

Most Jamaica-focused payroll software — including PayrollJamaica — falls in the low to mid range. Enterprise-tier pricing applies to providers that bundle payroll with full HR, benefits administration, and dedicated account management.

Annual Pricing

Some providers offer a discount of 10–20% for annual prepayment. If you're confident you'll stay with the software for 12 months, an annual plan typically saves 1–2 months of fees.

What's Usually Included in the Base Price

A standard Jamaica payroll software subscription should include:

  • Unlimited payroll runs per month
  • Automatic PAYE, NIS, NHT, Education Tax calculations
  • Digital payslips (emailed to employees or downloadable)
  • Monthly S01 remittance report for TAJ
  • Annual S01 return support
  • Rate updates when TAJ or the government changes thresholds
  • Basic reporting (payroll summary, deduction totals)

Premium tiers typically add:

  • Multi-company or multi-location payroll
  • HR features (leave management, employee records)
  • Custom pay types (commission, bonus, allowances)
  • API or accounting software integration
  • Dedicated support

Hidden Costs to Watch For

When evaluating payroll software pricing in Jamaica, ask specifically about:

  • Setup fees: Some providers charge J$10,000–30,000 to configure your account. PayrollJamaica has no setup fee.
  • Per-payslip fees: A few providers charge per payslip generated rather than a flat monthly rate. This can balloon costs for weekly payrolls.
  • Annual return support: Some base plans exclude assistance with the March 31 annual S01 return — you'd pay extra or handle it yourself.
  • Contract lock-in: Avoid providers requiring 12–24 month contracts without a clear exit clause.
  • Rate update lag: If the provider doesn't update rates quickly after a Budget announcement, you could be calculating incorrectly for weeks. Ask how fast they push rate changes.

Comparing Payroll Software to Accounting Firm Costs

This is the most common calculation Jamaican business owners do:

Option10 employees, monthly costAnnual cost
Accounting firm payrollJ$35,000–60,000J$420,000–720,000
PayrollJamaica softwareFrom J$6,000From J$72,000
Savings with softwareJ$29,000–54,000/monthJ$348,000–648,000/year

For a 10-person payroll, most businesses save J$350,000–650,000 per year by switching from accounting firm fees to PayrollJamaica. That's money that goes back into the business.

Is Free Payroll Software an Option in Jamaica?

There are no fully-featured, free payroll solutions built for Jamaica compliance. Spreadsheet templates can handle basic calculations but:

  • You must update thresholds manually every Budget cycle
  • There's no automated S01 report generation
  • There's no audit trail — critical if TAJ ever audits you
  • Errors compound over months and can cost far more than software fees

Some providers offer free trials (PayrollJamaica offers a free trial with no credit card required), which is the right way to test whether a solution fits your workflow before committing.

How to Choose the Right Price Point

Use this framework:

  1. Count your employees — most software prices by headcount
  2. Identify your must-haves — if you need multi-location or HR features, budget for a higher tier
  3. Calculate your current payroll processing cost — include accounting firm fees, your own time (hours × hourly value), and any penalties from past errors
  4. Compare to software pricing — the break-even is usually at 3–5 employees for most providers
  5. Test before you buy — run at least one real payroll during a trial period

For most Jamaican small businesses — especially those under 30 employees — dedicated payroll software is both the most affordable and most reliable option for staying TAJ-compliant.
